prompt: Camera move left, pull out, then pan left. An alpine lake sits beneath a wall of dark granite and lingering spring snow, its near shoreline edged with rounded glacial stones visible through clear, green-blue shallows. Small wind ripples travel across the surface while a colder inlet stream fans into the basin, bending reflections of fir trees and fractured cliff faces into trembling bands. At the far end, a narrow waterfall descends in stages, throwing pale mist that drifts low over the water. Layered ridges recede into bluish haze, and scattered clouds open brief patches of sun that brighten the wet rocks and reveal depth across the entire basin.

Camera move right, pull out, then pan right. A broad tundra valley in late autumn stretches beneath a fast-changing sky, with ochre grasses, shallow braided channels, and isolated stands of leafless birch rooted in frost-stiff ground. Low clouds race over distant mountains, opening a narrow seam of amber evening light that sweeps across the plain while a darker rain curtain hangs over the far ridgeline. Patches of old snow persist in gullies and north-facing folds, catching the last brightness. Wet earth and pooled water mirror the sky in broken fragments, and faint wind combs the sedges, making the transition from storm gloom to cold sunset feel gradual and physically real.

Camera push in. A desert badlands formation rises from a maze of dry washes and talus fans, revealing stacked sedimentary layers in rust, chalk, and muted violet. Erosion has carved the central butte into terraces, vertical seams, alcoves, and thin fins that cast sharp afternoon shadows, while scattered boulders at its base show fresh fracture faces and angular debris trails. Beyond it, mesas step back in receding planes through heat-softened air, each rim more faded than the last. Sparse shrubs cling to pockets of soil between exposed strata, and the hard ground shows braided runoff marks, making the geology legible from foreground cracks to distant escarpments.

Camera pull out. A sheltered sea cave opens onto a rocky cove at low tide, where translucent water folds over ribbed sand and dark basalt shelves slick with algae. Near the entrance, each small swell glides in as a thin green sheet, then breaks against uneven stone and bursts into white spray that hangs briefly in the cool air. Tide pools trapped in hollows reflect the bright mouth of the cave and the pale sky beyond, while submerged pebbles and amber-brown seaweed remain visible beneath the surface. Outside, a curving shoreline of black rock and scattered driftwood leads toward distant cliffs softened by marine haze, giving strong depth and clear shoreline structure.
